title: NVIM Basics
html:
NVIM Basics

Modes

Insert before cursor

i

Insert after cursor

a

Insert at line start

I

Insert at line end

A

New line below, insert

o

New line above, insert

O

Visual (char)

v

Visual (line)

V

Visual Block

Ctrl+v

Back to Normal

`Esc` / `Ctrl+[`

Command mode

:

Navigation

Left / Down / Up / Right

h j k l

Next / prev word

`w` / `b`

End of word

e

Start of line / first non-blank

`0` / `^`

End of line

$

Top / bottom of file

`gg` / `G`

Previous / next blank line (paragraph jump)

`{` / `}`

Half page down / up

`Ctrl+d` / `Ctrl+u`

Full page down / up

`Ctrl+f` / `Ctrl+b`

Jump to matching bracket

%

Search word under cursor (forward)

*

Search word under cursor (backward)

#

Go to definition / first occurrence

gd

Search

Search forward

/pattern

Search backward

?pattern

Next / previous match

`n` / `N`

Clear search highlight

:noh

Search for IP prefix (escape the dot)

/192\.168

Match any IP address

/^\d\+\.\d\+\.\d\+\.\d\+

Basic Editing

Delete char under cursor

x

Delete line

dd

Delete to end of line

D

Yank (copy) line

`yy` / `Y`

Paste after / before

`p` / `P`

Undo

u

Redo

Ctrl+r

Replace single char with c

r{c}

Change word

cw

Change to end of line

C

Change entire line

cc

Indent / de-indent line

`>>` / `<<`

Repeat last change

.

Save & Quit

Save

:w

Quit

:q

Save and quit

`:wq` / `ZZ`

Quit without saving

`:q!` / `ZQ`

Save all buffers

:wa

Save all and quit

:wqa

Reload file from disk (discard changes)

:e!

Files & Buffers

Open file

:e filename

Open in vertical split

:vs filename

Open in horizontal split

:sp filename

Switch between splits

Ctrl+w w

Move to split left/down/up/right

Ctrl+w hjkl

Next / previous buffer

`:bn` / `:bp`

List open buffers

:ls

Switch to buffer n

:b {n}

Close (delete) buffer

:bd

New tab

:tabnew

Next / previous tab

`gt` / `gT`

Pentesting Quick Edits

Edit /etc/hosts (requires sudo write trick)

# Open with sudo from shell
sudo nvim /etc/hosts

# Or from inside nvim, write with sudo tee
:w !sudo tee %

Add target entry pattern:

10.10.11.20    target.htb
10.10.11.20    api.target.htb
10.10.11.20    admin.target.htb

Search and Replace IP

" Replace all occurrences in file
:%s/192\.168\.10\./192.168.20./g

" With confirmation prompt
:%s/192\.168\.10\./192.168.20./gc

" Replace only in visual selection (select first with V)
:'<,'>s/10\.10\.11\.20/10.10.11.21/g

" Replace specific CIDR
:%s/10\.129\.\d\+\.\d\+/TARGET_IP/g

Delete Lines Matching Pattern (clean up lists)

" Delete all blank lines
:g/^$/d

" Delete lines NOT containing an IP
:g!/\d\+\.\d\+\.\d\+\.\d\+/d

" Delete lines containing a word (e.g. remove comments)
:g/#/d

" Delete lines containing 'closed'
:g/closed/d

Keep Only Lines Matching Pattern

" Keep only lines with IPs (delete everything else)
:v/\d\+\.\d\+\.\d\+\.\d\+/d

" Keep only lines with open ports
:v/open/d

" Keep only lines with hostnames (.htb)
:v/\.htb/d

Sort and Deduplicate IP List

" Sort lines
:%!sort

" Sort and remove duplicates
:%!sort -u

" Sort numerically (for IPs)
:%!sort -t. -k1,1n -k2,2n -k3,3n -k4,4n

" Remove blank lines then sort unique
:g/^$/d | %!sort -u

Extract Field from Colon-Separated List (e.g. /etc/passwd)

" Show only usernames (field 1) from passwd-style file
:%!awk -F: '{print $1}'

" Show usernames and shells
:%!awk -F: '{print $1, $7}'

" Extract just IPs from nmap output
:%!grep -oP '\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+'
